t_ch: 9-<unbreak>15</unbreak>-7…保险箱旁不知被谁刻了这样一组数字，你试了试，金属锁扣岿然不动。
t_en: 9-<unbreak>15</unbreak>-7... Someone carved these numbers into the side of the safe. You try the combination, but the metal lock still won't budge.

t_ch: 「被遗弃…我们…出不去…」细小的嘈杂声，从大脑深处窸窸窣窣地钻出，敲击在脆弱的鼓膜上，「留下来吧…这里太空了…留下吧……」
t_en: "Abandoned... We... We can't get out..." A soft cacophony, rustling from deep within your mind and knocking on your fragile eardrums. "Stay with us... It's too lonely here... Stay..."

t_ch: 已失去使用价值的电视，如抱团取暖般堆砌着，仿佛要对抗孤独带来的寒冷。
t_en: Televisions that have lost their value. They are all piled up, as if huddling together to fend off the chills from loneliness.

t_ch: 你盯着成堆的电视，感觉无数只眼睛也在同时盯着你。
t_en: You stare at the pile of televisions, and feel like there are countless eyes staring at you at the same time.

t_ch: 水幕如帘，波光映亮了墙壁。鲸群自远方巡游，像是要冲入头顶的另一片天地。
t_en: A cascading curtain, where a glistening light of waves illuminates the walls. A pod of whales is swimming in the distance, as if trying to break through the ceiling of the sky.

t_ch: 一切都颠倒了过来：瀑布逆流而上，鲸群奔向梦境的裂口，仿佛要坠入深渊，又像是在追逐光明。
t_en: Everything is upside down: Waterfalls flow upwards, the pod of whales rushing towards the breach in the Dreamscape, as if trying to dive into the abyss, but at the same time chasing after the light.

t_ch: 这架望远镜内播放着苏乐达的系列广告，而且不断循环，似乎永无休止。
t_en: The telescope displays an endless loop of SoulGlad commercials.

t_ch: 这架望远镜透出了正对面的苏乐达工厂大楼，清晰无比——总算有个能用的望远镜了。
t_en: This telescope is functional and provides a clear view into the SoulGlad factory building.

t_ch: 只是…它似乎没有放大功能。你左看右看，觉得还是直接用双眼欣赏这副景象更好。
t_en: However... it lacks magnification. Enjoying the view with your naked eyes seems preferable.

t_ch: 「您正在呼叫的是，『蓝调的时刻』。愿您在梦泡之海的舞会上被浪漫垂青。」
t_en: "You are calling the Blue Hour. May you find romantic encounters at the ball in the Ocean of Dream Bubbles."

t_ch: 「您正在呼叫的是，『薄暮的时刻』。愿您在匹诺康尼拍卖会中收获心仪的宝物。」
t_en: "You are calling the Moment of Dusk. May you find your desired treasures at the Penacony Auction Hall."

t_ch: 「您正在呼叫的是，『星辰的时刻』。愿您在机动球大赛中旗开得胜、一车当先。」
t_en: "You are calling the Moment of Stars. May you emerge victorious in the Roboball Contest."

t_ch: 「您正在呼叫的是，『太阳的时刻』。『初醒图书馆』将随时对您敞开大门。」
t_en: "You are calling the Moment of Sol. The gates to the Primal Waking Library are always open to you."

t_ch: 「您正在呼叫的是，『绿洲的时刻』。愿您在日光公园的午后尽享安恬。」
t_en: "You are calling the Moment of Oasis. Please enjoy a peaceful afternoon at Sunshine Park."

t_ch: <color=#eb4d3dff>「不要打给『安谧的时刻』！重复一遍，不要打给『安谧的时刻』！那里不对外开放！」</color>
t_en: <color=#eb4d3dff>"Don't call the Moment of Serenity! I repeat, do not call the Moment of Serenity! It's off-limits!"</color>

t_ch: 你敲了敲三月七的房门，里面毫无反应，可能她这会儿正在某个梦境时刻里四处拍照呢。
t_en: You knock on the door to March 7th's room, but no one responds. She's probably busy snapping photos in one of the Dreamscape moments.

t_ch: 属于熟悉女孩子的房间，不过这次没有对你敞开大门。
t_en: The room belongs to a girl with whom you are close, but the door isn't open to you this time.

t_ch: 一股浓郁的咖啡味从屋内传来…这可不方便入梦啊。
t_en: A rich aroma of coffee wafts from within the room... It seems to be a bad time for a dream.

t_ch: 你原打算敲敲姬子的房间门，但终究没有忍心去打扰她。
t_en: You planned to knock on the door to Himeko's room but decided not to bother her in the end.

t_ch: 你试着拽了拽门把手——纹丝不动，看来杨叔的安全意识很高。
t_en: You give the knob a twist, but the door doesn't budge. Looks like Mr. Yang has a high safety awareness.

t_ch: 瓦尔特的房间，散发着一股莫名「沉重」的气息，足以吓退那些不怀好意的宾客。
t_en: Welt's room gives off a somewhat somber aura, which is enough to intimidate guests who are up to no good.

t_ch: 一扇紧锁的房门，门后传来浓郁的香气，不知是来自花朵还是高档香水…或许你需要某些凭证才能将它打开。
t_en: A thick and heady fragrance drifts from the space behind the firmly locked door. You wonder whether it is the scent of flowers or a premium perfume... Perhaps, you need some sort of permit to unlock the door.

t_ch: 一扇紧锁的房门，门后传来轻微的电流声。直觉告诉你，强行打开这扇门可能会有坏事发生。
t_en: A subtle buzzing sound of electricity comes from the space behind the firmly locked door. Your instinct tells you that opening the door by force might lead to unthinkable consequences.

t_ch: 科琳的房间。除非她想出来，不然没人进得去——你已经知道那什么「锁闭式次元力场发生器」的存在了。
t_en: Colleen's room. No one can open the door unless she opens it herself. You are aware of the enclosed dimensional force field generator on the door.

t_ch: 一扇紧锁的房门。屋内没有传来任何声音，紧锁的门又在暗示它确实有人居住。
t_en: A firmly locked door. The room is quiet, but the fact that the door is locked indicates that it is occupied.

t_ch: 查德威克的房间，也是他人生中曾经历过的众多监牢之一。
t_en: Chadwick's room. It is also one of the many prisons he's lived in his lifetime.

t_ch: 这扇房门不会再度开启了——它已经封存了太多东西。
t_en: This door will never be opened again. Too many things are sealed in this room.

t_ch: 一扇紧锁的房门，门后传来淡淡的灰尘气息，似乎已经很久没有打扫——这并不奇怪，总有人不愿从梦中醒来。
t_en: A faint smell of dust comes from the space behind the firmly locked door, suggesting that the room has not been cleaned for quite some time. This is not unusual, as there are people who refuse to wake up from the dream.

t_ch: 正在打扫中的房间，很快就会迎来下一位追逐美梦的住客。
t_en: Cleaning is in progress. The room will soon welcome a new guest.

t_ch: 视野绝佳的观景台，几乎能将整个筑梦边境的景色尽收眼底。
t_en: The observation deck with breathtaking views. You can almost see the entire Dream's Edge at a glance.

t_ch: 这确实是个不容易发现的地方。向你介绍这里的人一定非常珍视你们的关系、才会把这幅美景与你分享。
t_en: It's not an easy place to find. The person who introduced it to you truly values your friendship by sharing this view.

t_ch: 你回想起和那个女孩共度的时光，她柔软的笑容，她轻盈的步伐，以及她最后一刻、被那怪物斩杀时流下的泪。
t_en: You recall the times spent with that girl. Her gentle smile, her graceful steps, and the tears she shed in her last moments before she was murdered by that monster.

t_ch: 那个时候，她为什么要说「对不起」呢？她脑海中所想的会是什么呢？倘若你们动作再快一点的话，那傻丫头是不是就不用死了呢？
t_en: At that moment, why did she apologize? What was she thinking? If you had reacted faster, would that silly girl not have died?

t_ch: 一股酸涩从你心底油然而生，随后是愤怒的火焰——足以将最坚固的金属烧成灰烬。
t_en: A tinge of sourness wells from the depths of your heart, followed by a flame of anger — intense enough to reduce the strongest metal to ashes.

t_ch: 面朝栏杆与高楼，你握紧了拳头……
t_en: Facing the railings and skyscrapers, you clench your fists...

t_ch: 适合潜入、隐藏；不宜追逐战。
t_en: Suitable for infiltration and concealment; not suitable for pursuit.

t_ch: 如果说下水道是一座城市的地底迷宫，那么通风管道便是高居穹顶的幽邃秘境。
t_en: If the sewers are a city's underground labyrinth, then ventilation ducts are the dome's well-kept secret.

t_ch: 糖浆味的烟雾从通风口里飘出…看来梦里的排气也是香的。
t_en: Syrupy smoke wafts through the vents... Seems like the exhaust in dreams smells good too.

t_ch: 你朝管道里丢了块石子，许久都没有回声传来。看来从这里爬进去的计划不太现实。
t_en: You throw a stone into the pipe, but you can't hear the echo. It's not a realistic plan to crawl through the pipe.

t_ch: 高楼的换气装置，由于造型奇特，筑梦师们都叫它「调料罐」。
t_en: The Dreamweavers call the ventilators in the skyscrapers "seasoning boxes" due to their peculiar shape.

t_ch: 「无动力通风球」，在任何情况下都能保证建筑内外的空气流通。
t_en: The non-kinetic ventilation ball ensures air circulation within and around the building under all circumstances.

t_ch: ——请不要将手指伸进叶片的缝隙间，除非你想被强制送回现实酒店。
t_en: Please do not stick your fingers between the slits in the blades, unless you wish to be forced to return to the hotel in reality.

t_ch: 探照灯，顾名思义，以强大的光源在黑暗中探索照明——但这玩意在灯火不熄的城市中能有多大用处呢？
t_en: The searchlight, as its name implies, offers a powerful light source for exploration in the dark. Yet, how beneficial are they in a city with constant illumination?

t_ch: 这些探照灯最大的用处，就是测试新梦境的光线追踪是否有效。
t_en: The greatest use for these searchlights is to test the effectiveness of the new Dreamscape's ray tracing.

t_ch: 都市传说一则：在有些夜晚，这盏灯会在天空中投射出巨大的有翼哺乳动物剪影——这说明有一位暗夜英雄即将出手。
t_en: According to urban legend, on certain nights, this lamp casts the silhouette of a giant winged mammal in the sky — a signal that a superhero of the night is poised to strike.

t_ch: 这盏探照灯的外壳破旧，光线昏黄而苍凉，你很好奇它究竟这样守望了多久。
t_en: This searchlight's shell is old and worn, and its light is dim and weak. You wonder how long it's been standing watch.

t_ch: 此处宜人的环境适合短暂的休憩，或者摸鱼。
t_en: The serene surroundings are perfect for a short break, or to slack off.

t_ch: 「禁止踩踏灌木、攀爬喷泉！」
t_en: "No treading on shrubs. No climbing on fountains!"

t_ch: 注：物品仅供演示，最终效果请以实际梦境为准。
t_en: Note: Items are for demonstration purposes. For the final results, please refer to the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 「浇灌用水，不可直接饮用。」
t_en: "Irrigation water, not for consumption."

t_ch: 「请勿靠近，以防跌落。」
t_en: "Keep a safe distance to prevent potential falls."

t_ch: 这里的天窗很高，如果不小心掉下去，或许在现实中醒来时也得头痛好一会。
t_en: The skylight here is so high that if you were to accidentally fall in, you might wake up in reality with a headache.

t_ch: 温暖的光透过玻璃传来，里面似乎是为舞会准备的布景，但现在空无一人。
t_en: Warm rays of light penetrate in through the glass. A seeming stage is prepared for a dance performance, except there's naught a soul there.

t_ch: 「不要幻想从这里跳下去，里面的场景都还没做完。」来自某位筑梦师的留言。
t_en: "Don't harbor delusions of jumping down there. The set pieces aren't done yet." A note from a Dreamweaver.

t_ch: 一般而言，这种水塔里既不会有泡发的尸体，也不存在美人鱼。
t_en: Usually speaking, such water towers won't have corpses soaked in them, nor will they house mermaids.

t_ch: 不必怀疑，这玩意虽然叫做「水塔」，里面装的也都是苏乐达。
t_en: There's no need to inquire: This thing may be called a water tower, but it's all SoulGlad stored in here.

t_ch: 很难想象，曾经竟有不止一名游客试图在这块广告牌顶部蹦极。
t_en: It's hard to imagine that more than one tourist has bungee jumped off the top of this billboard.

t_ch: 「广告位招租，价高者优先。」
t_en: "Billboard space for rent. Highest bidders first."

t_ch: 等新梦境开放后，人们就会为这面广告牌争相竞价。
t_en: When the new Dreamscape goes public, many will clamor to bid for this billboard space.

t_ch: 集成式能源站，为整个筑梦边境的灯光提供支持。等地图正式开放后，这种建筑的数量会翻上几番。
t_en: An integrated power station that supports all the lights of the entire Dream's Edge. When the map goes public, such structures will surely exponentially grow in number.

t_ch: 为筑梦边境提供能源的小屋，总有人对它的原理感到好奇——最后他们要么打不开门，要么只能发现里面空无一物。
t_en: A little house that provides power for the Dream's Edge. There'll always be some who are curious about the principles behind its operation. Eventually, either they can't get the door open, or they'll find that there's nothing behind it.

t_ch: 家族监管局，负责监督整个筑梦边境的工程进展——顺带驱逐那些不该出现的家伙。
t_en: The Family's surveillance agency, charged with overseeing the entire Dream's Edge's construction, as well as driving out people who should not be there.

t_ch: 再狂野的想象也需要制约，这便是家族监管局的职责。
t_en: Even the wildest of imaginations must be curbed — that is the duty of The Family's surveillance agency.

t_ch: 为筑梦师们打造的休憩场所。经常有人在这里光明正大地摸鱼。
t_en: A lounge center built for the Dreamweavers. There are people openly playing hooky in here.

t_ch: 筑梦师们的脾气往往和名气成正比，所以当他们试图在某个问题上达成一致时，不论动口还是动手都不稀奇。
t_en: The tempers and renown of Dreamweavers are typically proportionate, which is why, when they try to come to consensus on a certain problem, it's not uncommon to see verbal debates turn to blows.

t_ch: 脑力工作需要更多的休息时间，这间酒吧就是专门为此而建。
t_en: Work of the mind requires even more rest, which is what this bar was specifically designed for.

t_ch: 门扉紧锁——这里似乎并不欢迎访客。
t_en: The door is tightly locked — this place seemingly does not welcome visitors.

t_ch: 你推了推，大门纹丝不动；又推了推——还是别浪费力气啦！
t_en: You push on it, but the door does not budge. You try again... Best not to waste your strength!

t_ch: 门锁着，这算是一种保护——毕竟没人能说清门后的空间有没有建造完毕。
t_en: The door is locked, which is a kind of protection in itself — no one is really clear on whether or not the construction behind the door is complete.

t_ch: 这是一架看起来能爬的梯子——你试了一下，发现它压根没有物理碰撞。
t_en: This looks like a ladder that can be scaled. You give it a shot, and notice that it has absolutely no physics collision.

t_ch: 梯子连接的建筑顶端还没有建造完成，任何踩上去的人都会坠落——或许这就是梯子不能爬的原因。
t_en: The roof of the building that the ladder is attached to is incomplete. Anyone who steps on it will fall... Perhaps this is the reason why the ladder can't be scaled.

t_ch: 某种预感告诉你，哪怕是梦境建成之后，这架梯子也不能爬。
t_en: A hunch tells you that even after this Dreamscape is built, you won't be able to climb this ladder.

t_ch: 视野绝佳的观景台，几乎能将整个筑梦边境的景色尽收眼底。
t_en: This observation deck has breathtaking views. You can almost see the entire Dream's Edge at a glance.

t_ch: 这确实是个不容易发现的地方。向你介绍这里的人一定非常珍视你们的关系、才会把这幅美景与你分享。
t_en: It's not an easy place to find. The person who introduced it to you truly values your friendship by sharing this view.

t_ch: 你与那女孩创造的回忆已经不胜枚举。无论快乐与否，它们都会像吃进肚里的橡木蛋糕卷一样，变成你身体的一部分。
t_en: The memories you created with that girl are too many to count. Whether they were happy or otherwise, they will become a part of you, like an Oak Cake Roll eaten and ending up in your belly.

t_ch: 先前的嘈杂情绪渐渐消失了，取而代之的是一抹平静，仿佛没有流星划过的、现实的夜空。
t_en: The previously mixed emotions have gradually disappeared, replaced by a palette of serenity, a night sky of reality unstreaked by shooting stars.

t_ch: 你明白，你和她都有要完成的使命，或在梦境、或在现实。为此你们不得不暂时分离，走向命运为各自安排的道路。
t_en: You know that both of you have a mission to complete, be it in the Dreamscape or reality. To this end, you two are forced to go separate paths, toward your own pre-ordained destinies.

t_ch: 等一切结束后，你们还会再见的——你如此相信着。希望那时她一切安好。
t_en: When all of this is over, you will see each other again — that is what you believe. You hope she will be safe and well then.

t_ch: 视野绝佳的观景台，几乎能将整个筑梦边境的景色尽收眼底。
t_en: The observation deck with breathtaking views. You can almost see the entire Dream's Edge at a glance.

t_ch: 这确实是个不容易发现的地方。向你介绍这里的人一定非常珍视你们的关系、才会把这幅美景与你分享。
t_en: It's not an easy place to find. The person who introduced it to you truly values your friendship by sharing this view.

t_ch: 就像每本书都有最后一页，你在匹诺康尼的故事也迎来了落幕。于结尾前的高潮部分，那女孩化作了天上的流星。
t_en: Just as every book has its final page, your story in Penacony has come to an end. In the climactic moments leading up to the conclusion, the girl became a shooting star in the sky.

t_ch: 如今，「开拓」的道路再次分散出千百种可能性，是时候重新踏上旅途了。无论你选择去往何处，楼顶的这片天空都将永远映在你的梦中。
t_en: Now, the Path of Trailblaze is once again brimming with countless possibilities. It is time to embark on a new journey. No matter where you're headed, the sky above the building will always be etched in your dreams.

t_ch: 这景色，还挺漂亮的…你知道，它不是虚假的幻影。
t_en: You know, this breathtaking view in front of your eyes... is definitely not an illusion.

t_ch: 毕竟，栏杆尽头的远方是一片黎明。
t_en: After all, there is a dawn waiting at the end of the railing, visible in the distance.

t_ch: 茶几上的茶具们开始微微颤动…它们仿佛有话要说。
t_en: The tea utensils on the tea set start to tremble... as if they have something to say.

t_ch: 「我们得从这里离开！■■就要打进来了！」
t_en: "We have to get out of here! ■■ is about to breach this place!"

t_ch: 「混蛋，不要散布紧张情绪！我们有人，也有武器，可以应付……」
t_en: "Fool, stop spreading anxiety! We've got men and weapons. We'll manage..."

t_ch: 「就凭我们，你疯了吗？！■■会把我们撕成碎片！」
t_en: "With just us? Are you crazy!? ■■ will tear us to shreds!"

t_ch: 「没什么…这没什么，我们可是亡命之徒，记得吗？现在给我坐下，喝了这杯…我们应付得来，我们绝对能挺过去……」
t_en: "It's nothing... This is nothing. We're desperados on the lam, remember? Now sit down and drink this... We'll manage, and we'll definitely overcome this..."

t_ch: 你被眼前的奇景所吸引，攫取了全部心神；
t_en: You're mesmerized by the wondrous sight before you — it robs you of your full attention.

t_ch: 沉重脚步拖拽着你前进，就像是夜蛾赴火；
t_en: Heavy footsteps drag toward you up ahead, like moths to a flame.

t_ch: 灵魂正催促你抛弃苦果，跨过这无形之门；
t_en: Souls urge you to unburden your woes and step through this formless door.

t_ch: 而后你的一切去伪存真，与古老之梦共存。
t_en: Where you will shed your falsities and embrace the truth, co-existing with the ancient dream.

t_ch: 只可惜——哦不，应该说可喜可贺——你根本过不去。
t_en: Alas... Or rather, congratulations — you can't go through at all.

t_ch: 「通过我，将进入混沌之梦」
t_en: "Step past me and enter the Dream of Chaos"

t_ch: 「通过我，将进入亘古之虚空」
t_en: "Step past me and enter the Immemorial Void"

t_ch: 「通过我，将进入漆黑之活塚」
t_en: "Step past me and enter the Living Grave of Dark"

t_ch: 「在我之先，无物实存，唯不朽者不朽」
t_en: "Before me, there was nothing, apart from the immortal"

t_ch: 「于我之下，万物流转，方嬗变者嬗变」
t_en: "After me, everything flowed, when all evolved"

t_ch: 你望向「尽头」，那里似乎有什么无法窥见的存在，正于永恒静默之中，吟唱着不可言说的赞美诗。
t_en: You gaze at the "End" — there appears to be some unseen presence there, singing an unspeakable hymn in the ever-silence.

t_ch: 架子鼓正在呜咽，你知道这种乐器在现实中绝不可能发出近似的声音。
t_en: The drum kit whimpers — you know that this instrument would never make a sound remotely close to this in reality.

t_ch: 「…我们不会抛下他…我们会在一起…一直一直……」
t_en: "...We won't abandon him... We will... always, always... together..."

t_ch: 「哭吧！尽情哭吧！■■可不会放过我们——我们都是犯过错的罪人！我们是犯过错的罪人！」
t_en: "Cry! Cry out all you want! ■■ will never let us off! We are sinners who have sinned! We are sinners who have sinned!"

t_ch: 「谁来救救他…谁来救救我们……」
t_en: "Who will save him... Who will save us..."

t_ch: 「谁也不会来！谁也不会来！罪人不配拥有救星，只配被■■永远奴役！」
t_en: "No one will come! No one will come! Sinners do not deserve saviors, and they only deserve to be enslaved by ■■!"

t_ch: 在你靠近的一瞬，台子上的锅碗瓢盆通通躁动起来。恍惚间，你似乎听到了模糊的人声……
t_en: The moment you get near, the pots and pans on the counter move about restlessly. Suddenly, you seem to hear a muffled human voice...

t_ch: 嗝…来喝一杯吧，苏乐达…它真的很好喝！
t_en: *gulp*... Come have a drink, SoulGlad... It's really tasty!

t_ch: 现在？你怕不是在开玩笑吧？
t_en: Now? You must be kidding me, right?

t_ch: 等一下…我记得你！之前在大厅里，那群囚犯都围着你欢呼，难道你就是他们的头头？
t_en: Hold on... I remember you! The surrounding convicts were cheering you on at the lobby. Could you be their leader?

t_ch: …今天是个快乐的日子，我先…敬你们一杯。朋友们，为了健康！
t_en: ...Today's a joyous day. Let me first... raise my glass to you all. To health, my friends!

t_ch: 快乐的日子？呵，对你们来说或许是，不过……
t_en: A joyous day? Heh, it might be to you, but...

t_ch: 一声枪响，随后是重物落地的声音。似乎是怕那囚犯还有口气，狱警又接着补了几枪。
t_en: A gunshot rings out, followed by a heavy thud. Probably afraid that the prisoner is still breathing, the warden adds a few more shots to finish the job.

t_ch: 这群囚犯天天喝那什么甜汽水，把脑子都喝傻了…喂，你们几个，过来把地面清理干净！
t_en: What's that sweet soda these convicts are drinking all day? It's rotting their brains... Hey, you lot, clean up this floor!

t_ch: 回过神来时，你感觉怒火已经填满了胸膛。
t_en: When you come to your senses, you feel like rage is exploding from your chest.

t_ch: 触碰到这簇忆泡时，一股浓郁的、莫名的情绪淹没了你。
t_en: When you touch this memory bubble cluster, an intense, inexplicable emotion drowns you.

t_ch: 破碎的画面一闪而过。你看到了离别，听到了哭声，尝到了嘴角咸咸的液体。
t_en: Fragments of images flash by. You see parting, hear crying, and taste salty liquid in the corner of your mouth.

t_ch: 当你从这情绪中回过神来时，一滴泪已经流到了耳边……
t_en: When you recover from this emotion, a single tear has reached the side of your ear...

t_ch: 喷泉中汩汩流淌的梦泡对你说着悄悄话：「真实与存在都是伪命题，快跳进来吧——和我们、和这个梦融为一体！」
t_en: The dream bubble blubbering from the fountain whispers to you: "Truth and existence are false propositions. Hurry up and jump in — become one with us in this dream!"

t_ch: 靠近这座喷泉时，你感觉到一阵恍惚。巨大的梦泡仿佛长出了眼睛和双手……
t_en: When you move near this fountain, you feel a wave of stupor. A giant dream bubble appears to have grown eyes and hands...

t_ch: 「逐梦客，把我喝掉！」 它指向自己饱满的身躯，热情地发出邀请……
t_en: "Dreamchaser, drink me!" It points to its own bubbling body, warmly extending an invitation...

t_ch: 头顶那巨型茶杯的大眼睛不再滴溜溜乱转——此刻它正紧紧盯着你。
t_en: The huge eyes atop the giant teacup stop darting around profusely — it is now fervently staring at you.

t_ch: 「喝了它，这是逐梦客的命运！」它指着喷泉里的梦泡，聒噪地在你脑子里大喊大叫。
t_en: "Drink it. This is the destiny of a Dreamchaser!" It points to the dream bubble in the fountain, noisily yelling in your head.

t_ch: 可…代价是什么呢？
t_en: But... What is the price?

t_ch: 平平无奇的储物架。上面没有子弹、火柴、草药…洗手液或是灌装凝胶。
t_en: An ordinary storage shelf. There are no bullets, matches, herbs on it... nor are there hand sanitizers or filling gel.

t_ch: 恍惚中那蓝色蝴蝶向你飞来，翅膀挥动间洒下艳丽却致命的鳞片。
t_en: The azure butterfly flits towards you, its wings scattering splendid, fatal scales.

t_ch: 你想要尖叫、想要哀嚎，声音却随着血液与以太一同冻结，等待你的唯有静谧的死亡与深不见底的绝望……
t_en: You want to scream, to howl, but your voice is frozen with blood and ether, awaiting your serene death and unfathomable despair...

t_ch: 然而片刻之后，你清醒过来，长舒一口气——相框中安静地匍匐着一只死去已久的普通蝴蝶，而你也并非什么黑魔法师。
t_en: And then in an instant, you awaken, inhaling a long drag of air — in the picture frame, an ordinary long-dead butterfly sleeps, and you're no magician of the dark arts.

t_ch: 人们说书是记忆的有形载体。但在充斥着梦泡的酒店中，这一墙书本就显得相形见绌了。
t_en: People say books are the physical medium of memories, but in the hotel filled with dream bubbles, this wall of books looks puny in comparison.

t_ch: 在名为「匹诺康尼」的梦中，被梦见的人醒了。
t_en: In the dream called "Penacony," the person who was being dreamed about rouses.

t_ch: 你曾在此醒来。如今你感到好奇，倘若于这破碎梦中就此睡去，再次睁开眼时自己将出现在哪里？
t_en: You once woke up here. Now you are curious: If you sleep here in this fragmented dream, where would you appear when you once again open your eyes?

t_ch: 形似贝壳的深眠装置，质感奢华。躺进其中之后，催眠气体便会包裹你的全身。
t_en: A shell-shaped deep-sleep device, luxuriously crafted. When you lie down inside, hypnotic gases will envelop your body.

t_ch: 皮革沙发。光滑、柔软，香橙果冻一般Q弹。
t_en: A leather sofa — glossy and sleek, soft, and bouncy like orange jelly.

t_ch: 舒适的沙发。适合在上面尽情舒展、来回翻滚，就好似小丑鱼在艳丽珊瑚丛中嬉戏——只可惜你根本坐不下去。
t_en: A comfy sofa. Perfect for stretching out, or rolling about, like a clownfish playfully weaving about colorful coral. A pity you can't even sit on it.

t_ch: 杯中空无一物，不知何日才能再度填满。
t_en: The cup is empty, not knowing which day it will be full again.

t_ch: 闲置的茶具整齐摆在桌面上，曾经的欢谈情景仿佛还历历在目。
t_en: The idle tea set is neatly arranged on the table, conversations and sights of past still vivid in one's mind.

t_ch: 空荡荡的前台，像现实里那样门庭若市的情景怕是再也看不见了。
t_en: An empty front desk — bustling crowds of reality will probably not be seen.

t_ch: 这里是家族的安全区，可以抵御忆域迷因的袭击。
t_en: This is The Family's safe zone that can fend off Memory Zone Meme attacks.

t_ch: 即便不再有人需要办理入住，家族依旧妥善保养着梦中的柜台，以免某天需要将它用作最后的防线。
t_en: Even if there's no one else to check in, The Family still maintains the reception in the dream, in case it needs to be used as the last line of defense one day.

t_ch: 酒店公用的梦境电话，可以打给不同「时刻」…不知它还能不能用了。
t_en: The hotel's public dreamphone that can call different Moments... Is it still operational?

t_ch: 你拿起听筒，里面只传来无意义的尖鸣声。
t_en: You pick up the receiver, and all you can hear is meaningless static.

t_ch: 破碎的梦境失了惯常的引力，就连循规蹈矩的行李都开始变得放荡不羁。
t_en: The fragmented dream has lost its usual allure, and even the orderly baggage has begun to stray awry.

t_ch: 「来跳舞吗？」黄金的行李架提议，「和我们一起、和头顶的鲸群一起……」
t_en: "Dance?" A golden suitcase proposes, "With us, with the pod of whales above..."

t_ch: 行李飞向天空，如同骏鹰挣脱枷锁。
t_en: The suitcase flies into the sky, like a hawk breaking free of its fetters.

t_ch: 「嘿，你知道吗！」古怪的屏幕兴致勃勃地叫住了路过的你，「从现在起，我『免费』啦！」
t_en: "Hey, did you know!" The odd screen excitedly stops you as you pass, "From now on, I'm free!"

t_ch: 梦泡如虫茧般附在这面屏幕上，使其肮脏如污泥。
t_en: Dream bubbles are plastered on this screen like insect cocoons, making it dirty as sludge.

t_ch: 屏幕上显示出一些不太「常规」的网站入口，你不会想要点进去的。
t_en: A few strange web interfaces are shown on the screen. You don't really want to click into them.

t_ch: 梦泡如虫茧般附在这面屏幕上，使之模糊如锈铁。
t_en: Dream bubbles are plastered on this screen like insect cocoons, making it blurry as rusted metal.

t_ch: 球笼服务已暂时停运，恢复日期尚未确定。
t_en: The spheroid service has been halted indefinitely.

t_ch: 这种时候还有谁会想要使用这些载具呢——惊梦剧团、还是忆域迷因？
t_en: Who would even use these vehicles at a time like this — the Dreamjolt Troupe, or the Memory Zone Memes?

t_ch: 被污染的梦境入口，如同一只从深渊中睁开的眼睛。
t_en: The tainted Dreamscape entrance, akin to an open eye in the abyss.

t_ch: 「不要靠近那圈光环，否则你将失去自我。」一个声音在你耳边低语道。
t_en: "Don't get close to that light circle, or you'll lose yourself." A low voice rumbles beside your ear.

t_ch: 几个梦泡占据了沙发前的空间，这些座位现在已经被禁止使用…不过，或许可以试试？
t_en: A few dream bubbles occupy the space in front of the sofa. These seats are now prohibited from usage... But, why not give it a whirl?

t_ch: 长沙发最适合睡觉了，尤其是你还可以拿那些梦泡当枕头。
t_en: The sofa is most appropriate for sleeping, especially when you can use these dream bubbles as pillows.

t_ch: 即使梦境的一滴水珠，落在座位上也是一场湍流。
t_en: Even a single water drop in the Dreamscape becomes a deluge when dropped on the seat.

t_ch: 梦泡带来的紊乱侵扰了这处座位，现在它已经不适合被任何人使用。
t_en: The disorder brought about by the dream bubbles have infested this seat — it is now unsuitable for usage.

t_ch: 人们说书是记忆的有形载体。但在充斥着梦泡的酒店中，这一墙书本就显得相形见绌了。
t_en: People say books are the physical medium of memories, but in the hotel filled with dream bubbles, this wall of books looks puny in comparison.

t_ch: 「看完记得放回原位！」当你触碰到某本书时，书的合页突然说出这么句话，吓得你赶紧抽回了手。
t_en: "Don't forget to put them back in the right spots when you're done!" When you touch a book, its spine suddenly blurts out a reminder, causing you to pull your hand back in shock.

t_ch: 曾经完整的圆桌，如今已四分五裂。
t_en: A once complete round table, now shattered into pieces.

t_ch: 或许从前有一大家人围在这条桌前共进晚餐，但现在他们和桌子都不再完整了。
t_en: Maybe there was a big family who ate dinner at this table, but now both they and the table are incomplete.

t_ch: 「…为什么…不嗨起来……」说罢，架子鼓发出了几声闷响，声音如同铁锤敲击墙壁。
t_en: "... Why... can't we get high" On that note, the drum set produces a few muffled notes, like a hammer knocking away at a wall.

t_ch: 「啦啦啦…噜噜噜…啦啦啦噜！」小号的声音如同垂死女人的尖叫。
t_en: "Lalala... Doodoodoo... Lalaladoo!" A trumpet's sound screams like a dying woman.

t_ch: 架子鼓不自然地抖动着，时不时传来低沉的吼声，仿佛有一只怪物正蒙在鼓里。
t_en: The drum kit unnaturally starts to shake, intermittently emitting low howling sounds, like a monster sealed in it.

t_ch: 「…到…最后…他也没有…离开…我们不会离开……」
t_en: "... Till... the last moment... he didn't... leave... We won't leave..."

t_ch: 一道欢快的嗓音在这空荡荡的宴会厅中炸开、回响：「诸位的掌声与喝彩是对我们最大的鼓励，谢谢大家——」
t_en: A cheerful voice explodes and reverberates through the empty banquet hall: "Your applause and cheers are the greatest motivation for us. Thank you, everyone..."

t_ch: 恍惚间你仿佛听见提琴低沉优雅的嗓音：「我们的演奏…您觉得好听…吗…？」
t_en: You seem to hear a low, elegant violin: "Did you like... our performance...?"

t_ch: 「好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好听吗好好好好好好好好好听吗——」
t_en: "Didyoulikedidyoulikedidyoulikedidyoulikedidyoulikedidyoulikedidyoulikedidyoulikedidyoulike—"

t_ch: 据说在梦境酒店还正常时，这个房间压根无需侍者、食物自己就能端上顾客的桌。
t_en: It's said that when the Hotel in the dreamscape was running, this room had no need for attendants — food would serve themselves on guests' tables.

t_ch: 相比于盐和胡椒，梦中最不可或缺的调料是爱。
t_en: Rather than salt and pepper, love is the key ingredient in a dream.

t_ch: 柜门后是梦境最为隐蔽的空间，非常适合躲藏——但时间久了还是会被忆域迷因抓出来。
t_en: Behind the cabinet door is the Dreamscape's most hidden space, very good for hiding. But even after a while, one would still be caught and dragged away by the Memory Zone Memes.

t_ch: 紧锁的柜门，它背后的秘密永远无法为人所知了。
t_en: A tightly-shut cabinet door, the secrets behind it forever locked away from anyone's knowledge.

t_ch: 全垒打，幸好没人站在下面。
t_en: A home run — thankfully no one was standing below.

t_ch: 吊灯深深嵌进了地板里，它的重击足以让人从梦中醒来十次。
t_en: The chandelier is deeply lodged into the floor, its heavy impact enough to awaken one from a dream ten times over.

t_ch: 屏幕上只余无意义的画面，伴随着刺耳的电流声。
t_en: Only garbled visuals are left on the screen, accompanied by the sounds of an ear-piercing electrical current.

t_ch: 荧幕投出的冷光让房间显得更为孤寂，可你怎么也关不上它。
t_en: The cold light projected from the screen showers the room with even more loneliness. You can't turn it off no matter what you do.

t_ch: 如果有什么东西想要从这堆屏幕里爬出来的话，它该不会迷路吧？
t_en: If something wanted to crawl out from those screens, it wouldn't get lost, would it?

t_ch: 当你望向这些屏幕的时候，它们背后或许有什么也在回望着你。
t_en: When you gaze at these screens, perhaps something behind them is gazing back at you.

t_ch: 这些屏幕上只有无意义的画面，伴随着刺耳的电流声。
t_en: Only garbled visuals are left on these screens, accompanied by the sounds of an ear-piercing electrical current.

t_ch: 记载于纸上的资料，效率比梦泡更低，但更加可靠。
t_en: Information recorded on paper may be less efficient than dream bubbles, but is far more reliable.

t_ch: 可以预见整理这些纸张会是件多么困难的事。
t_en: Tidying up these papers would be such a hassle.

t_ch: 巨大的梦泡淹没了这座电梯。倘若你不想被混乱的记忆填满，就离它远一点吧。
t_en: Giant dream bubbles have flooded this elevator. If you don't want to be drowning in chaotic memories, you'd better stay away from the elevator.

t_ch: 由于忆质的阻隔，酒店各层已经失去了联系，电梯自然也无法使用了。
t_en: Memoria isolation has caused all hotel floors to lose comms capability, which also means the elevator is naturally out of order.

t_ch: 梦泡，凝结的梦境精华。
t_en: Dream bubbles, made up of condensed Dreamscape essence.

t_ch: 是谁从梦境之河里舀出了它们？
t_en: Who scooped them up from the Dreamscape's River?

t_ch: 每团梦泡里的美梦都足以填满人的脑海——当然，噩梦也是。
t_en: Every dream in the dream bubbles is enough to fill a person's head — as can the nightmares.

t_ch: 指尖大小的一点便蕴含着人的一生。
t_en: Something the size of a fingertip is enough to contain a life's worth of experiences.

t_ch: 舒适的皮革沙发。可惜这里缺少能让它实现存在价值的对象。
t_en: A comfortable leather sofa. Unfortunately, it lacks an object that would help it realize its worth.

t_ch: 台前已无来宾，唯余座椅冰凉。
t_en: There are no guests at the front desk, and the seats have turned cold.

t_ch: 「干杯！」酒杯们在空中彼此相碰，假装出这里还有人时的热闹景象。
t_en: "Cheers!" The wine glasses are clinking in the air, pretending that there is still a bustling scene filled with people.

t_ch: 你看着雅座上空不安分的伞棚，不由想起每当台风天气出行，你与伞是如何艰难地领受大自然的馈赠。
t_en: You look at the canopy above the private seats and can't help but be reminded of travel during typhoon season — how you and your umbrella braved the ardors of nature's gift.

t_ch: 不知道从哪飘来的伞座，它的存在属实有些突兀，更恼人的是，你还搬不动它。
t_en: Not sure where the umbrella seat came from, its appearance is quite a surprise. What's more annoying is that you can't move it.

t_ch: 这才叫宴会——足够高调、足够热闹！而且…足够「嗨」！
t_en: This is what I call a party! It's high profile and lively enough, and it's definitely "high" enough!

t_ch: 「该死的重力！」飞得最低的那把椅子说。
t_en: "Darn gravity!" says the chair that's flying the lowest.

t_ch: 你尝试着打了一杆。啪——黑球应声入洞。看来你此前苦练出的技术还未生疏。
t_en: You try your hand at a bat. Whack! The black ball goes into the hole. Looks like you haven't lost your hard-earned skills.

t_ch: 你继续出杆。哎呀…糟糕，红球跑偏了。你想要一杆清台的愿望就此破灭。
t_en: You continue to bat. Oh dear... Oh no, the red ball went astray. Your hopes of a clean shot are dashed.

t_ch: 你继续出杆——反正没人对局，想怎么打是你的自由。但这次力道似乎大了些，蓝球撞到了天花板上。你很疑惑，先前从未发现自己的臂力是如此地惊人。
t_en: You continue to bat, since no one's playing, and you're free to hit however you want. But this time you use too much force, causing the blue ball to hit the ceiling. You're puzzled — you never noticed you had such a strong arm before.

t_ch: 你继续出杆。可这次…黄球仿佛发了疯似的在房间里飞舞起来！它的运动轨迹毫无规律，速度也快得惊人，最后径直砸进了你的上衣口袋里。
t_en: You continue to bat. But this time... The yellow ball goes nuts and starts dancing around the room! It's moving erratically at shocking speeds, and it ends up in your shirt pocket.

t_ch: …在打出第五杆前，你最好先祈祷物理学在梦境里也存在。
t_en: ...Before you bat your fifth shot, you better pray that physics exist in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 你在梦中，看见时间的轮盘变形融化，嘀嗒、嘀嗒——
t_en: In your dream, you witness the wheels of time morphing and melting, tick-tock, tick-tock.

t_ch: 扭曲的钟表，它所指的时刻和梦境本身一样混乱。
t_en: Twisted clocks, the moments they point to are as confusing as the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 倘若这是一个飞镖盘，那么它的上靶率肯定低到发指。
t_en: If this were a dartboard, it would have a ridiculously low hit rate.

t_ch: 欢迎光临「惊梦酒吧」。店主近期不在，营业时间另行通知。有事请留言。
t_en: Welcome to Dreamjolt Hostelry. The owner is currently away, and the business hours will be updated later. Please leave a message if you have any inquiries.

t_ch: 「呐…我想您呀，您听见了吗？」
t_en: "Hey... I miss you. Can you hear me?"

t_ch: 「回来吧酒吧店主，我最骄傲的团长，历历在目的糖浆，汽水依旧在流淌…」
t_en: "Please come back, bar owner. You're the leader that I'm the proudest of. The syrup is still there, and the soda is still flowing..."

t_ch: 「汪！我知道店主在哪，我们去把她找回来！！」
t_en: "Woof! I know where the bar owner is. Let's bring her back!"

t_ch: 欢迎光临「惊梦酒吧」。店主近期不在，营业时间另行通知。有事请留言。
t_en: Welcome to Dreamjolt Hostelry. The owner is currently away, and the business hours will be updated later. Please leave a message if you have any inquiries.

t_ch: 「呜呜…还没营业吗，还没营业吗。再不开门的话，我就要哭出来了哦？」
t_en: "Boohoo... Is the bar still closed? I'm going to cry if it doesn't resume operation."

t_ch: 「下次别再让我跑空了，你这不务正业的调饮师！」
t_en: "Don't make me come here for nothing again, you lazy bartender!"

t_ch: 「闭店期间，我会照顾怪物们。别担心。」
t_en: "I'll look after the monsters while the bar is closed. Don't worry."

t_ch: 本区域暂未开放，请不要擅自翻越护栏。
t_en: This area is closed. Please refrain from climbing over the velvet rope barrier.

t_ch: 禁止在酒吧内嬉戏打闹。违反者将被罚去清洁地板。
t_en: No fooling around within the bar premises. Violators will be punished to mop the floor.

t_ch: 空荡荡的舞台，倒也符合酒吧现在冷清的气氛。
t_en: An empty stage. It suits the currently desolate ambiance in the bar.

t_ch: 看起来昂贵的木制柜台，不知为何留下了许多齿痕与爪印。
t_en: An expensive-looking wooden counter. For some reason, it is covered with bite marks and claw prints.

t_ch: 你不禁开始想象，若是灯光骤然亮起，酒客推门而至，会是怎样热闹而欢快的场面。
t_en: You can't help picturing how lively and joyful the bar would be if the lights came on and the patrons walked in through the door now.

t_ch: 酒柜的装饰极为考究，每个细节都经过精心设计。那位店主的品味想必很不错。
t_en: The decoration on the liquor cabinet is exquisitely made. Every detail of the cabinet is well-designed. The bar owner must have great taste.

t_ch: 猎犬无神的眼底散发着品红色的光芒。这视线令你毛骨悚然。
t_en: The Hound's glassy eyes emit a sanguine glow, creating an eerie sensation.

t_ch: 正当你想要伸出手触碰这尊雕像时，它竟在转瞬之间消失得无影无踪。
t_en: As you attempt to touch the statue, it suddenly vanishes into thin air.

t_ch: 在这座充满欢闹与喧嚣的美梦中，这只猎犬选择保持沉默，冷眼旁观。
t_en: This Hound chooses to remain in silence amidst the lively and joyful dream.

t_ch: 发觉你已经注意到它，猎犬的身影一眨眼便消失了。
t_en: Upon realizing that you have noticed it, the Hound swiftly vanishes.

t_ch: 猎犬默默地注视着你和正在不断增殖的新生梦境——尽管它只是一尊雕像，并非真正的生命。
t_en: The Hound silently observes you and the burgeoning dreams — even though it is merely a statue.

t_ch: 当你和它四目相对时，这只猎犬飞也似地遁入忆域深处。
t_en: As your eyes meet, the Hound swiftly departs into the depths of the Memory Zone.

t_ch: 你从猎犬的视线中捕捉到一丝不易察觉的思绪——宽慰、孤独、哀伤…但为什么？
t_en: Faint traces of unspoken thoughts are discernible in the Hound's gaze — relief, loneliness, and sorrow... but why?

t_ch: 你陷入思索，不曾察觉这只苍老的猎犬已经杳无踪迹。
t_en: Lost in thought, you fail to notice the absence of the grizzled Hound.

t_ch: 你注意到一道冷冷的视线——原来是一尊猎犬雕像。
t_en: A cold presence catches your attention. It is a statue of a Hound.

t_ch: 有那么一瞬间，你惊觉这只猎犬的存在似乎变得不大*可靠*。下一秒，它便被扭曲的忆域吞没了。
t_en: For a moment, you are startled as the Hound's presence appears less *stable*. The next moment, it gets consumed by the distorted Memory Zone.

t_ch: 偌大的宅邸中，一条籍籍无名的猎犬正四处观瞧。
t_en: A noteless Hound roams the grand mansion, scanning its surroundings.

t_ch: 很快它便发现了你，随后变戏法般地消失在了屋檐下的阴影中。
t_en: Soon, it realizes your presence before vanishing into the shadows under the eaves.

t_ch: 猎犬的姿态与卡通动画中的哈努兄弟有几分神似——沉默寡言，恪尽职守——每一匹狼在被驯化之后都能获得如此可贵的品格。
t_en: The Hound's posture resembles that of Hanu from a cartoon — silent and loyal, a characteristic belonging to domesticated wolves.

t_ch: 机警的猎犬注意到了你，留下一道极具威胁意味的视线后便消失了。
t_en: The vigilant Hound notices your presence, casting a threatening glare before disappearing.

t_ch: 猎犬立于老人身侧，高昂着头颅，仿若昨日重现。
t_en: The Hound stands by the side of the old man, its head held high as if basked in its former glory.

t_ch: 它注视着你，笔直的身躯渐渐溶解于粘稠的忆质，眼底依旧迸射着怀疑一切的光。
t_en: It gazes at you, its solid form dissolving into the sticky memoria, and its eyes retaining a skeptical glow.

t_ch: 这尊雕像被放置在这里。或许它曾见证过盛会海选最欢乐的模样。
t_en: Perhaps, this statue has witnessed the most joyful moments in the auditions.

t_ch: 仿佛是察觉到自己的姿态与周遭格格不入，它很快消失在了你的眼前。
t_en: Sensing its incongruity with its surroundings, it quickly vanishes from your sight.

t_ch: 即便猎犬失去了踪影，那道令人不安的品红色视线依旧缠绕着你。
t_en: Even after the Hound has disappeared, the lingering sanguine sight continues to unsettle you.

t_ch: 看来它一定还在匹诺康尼的某处潜伏着——如果你想摆脱它的视线，恐怕得先找到它才行。
t_en: The Hound must still be lurking somewhere in Penacony. If you want to get rid of its gaze, you may need to locate its whereabouts first.

t_ch: 那道冰冷的视线彻底消失，你如释重负——这座金色的美梦里再也不会有猎犬踽踽独行的身影了。
t_en: The disturbing sight finally fades away, bringing you a sense of relief: The Hound no longer wanders around in this sweet dream.

t_ch: 啊，一位年轻的逐梦客。
t_en: Ah, it's a young Dreamchaser.

t_ch: 希望匹诺康尼的梦境给你带来了美好的体验，这是所有家族成员一致的心愿。
t_en: I hope you had a wonderful experience in Penacony's Dreamscape. After all, such is the wish of all Family members.

t_ch: 老人好像一眼便看穿了你的身份——显然，他知道你不该出现在这里。
t_en: The old man seems to have seen right through you. Obviously, he knows you shouldn't be here.

t_ch: 我…我在这里工作！
t_en: I—I earn my living here!

t_ch: 我不小心迷路了。
t_en: I just happened to lose my way.

t_ch: 惠特克爵士的嘴角露出浅浅的笑意，你从中还读出了一丝不屑。
t_en: There is a hint of disdain in the faint smile surfacing on Sir Whittaker's face.

t_ch: 有财力在匹诺康尼消费的贵宾往往都有一个共性：他们都很会撒谎。显然，你对这项技术并不擅长。
t_en: Those who're wealthy enough to spend in Penacony have something in common: They're all pretty good at lying. Obviously, it's a talent you don't have.

t_ch: 不过无需紧张，我并不觉得你出现在这里有何不妥。你是一个善良的人，尽管有时会鲁莽行事，打破规则…但那都影响不到你的本性。
t_en: There's no need to worry. I don't find your presence here inappropriate. You're a kind soul. Although you sometimes act recklessly and tend to break rules, none of those changes your true nature.

t_ch: 很多人都没有意识到，我们在梦境中设立的规则并非严格的法律，它们更像是一些…「建议」。
t_en: What many don't realize is that the rules we established in the Dreamscape aren't strict laws. They're more like... "recommendations."

t_ch: 越线的行动并不一定意味着会受到惩罚；而需要被制裁的行为在仍处于「念头」的阶段就应当被制止——这就是家族的行事方式。
t_en: When you do something that crosses the line, it doesn't necessarily result in punishment. In fact, behaviors that deserve punishment should have been nipped in the bud when they are still an "idea" — that's how The Family handles things.

t_ch: 你究竟是谁？
t_en: Who exactly are you?

t_ch: 一位谦恭的家族成员，匹诺康尼所有宾客的朋友。仅此而已。
t_en: A humble Family member and a friend to all Penacony's guests — that's it.

t_ch: 你可以继续上路，也可以提出问题。盛会之梦会响应你的所有诉求。
t_en: You can continue your journey or ask questions. The Dream of Festivities will respond to all your requests.

t_ch: 我想和你了解一些事。
t_en: There are some things I want to find out more about.

t_ch: 贵宾启口，我定知无不言。
t_en: I'll tell you everything I know, honored guest.

t_ch: 你在这里做什么？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 「观察」——这就是我在做的事。
t_en: Observation — that's what I'm doing.

t_ch: 听上去怪轻松的。
t_en: Sounds pretty easy to me.

t_ch: 惠特克爵士微微点头，似乎默认了你的说法。
t_en: Sir Whittaker seems to acknowledge your words with a slight nod.

t_ch: 所以…等于什么都不干？
t_en: So... you're basically doing nothing, right?

t_ch: 惠特克爵士微微点头，似乎默认了你的说法。
t_en: Sir Whittaker seems to acknowledge your words with a slight nod.

t_ch: 梦境里本就没有什么难事，只是人们都被自己的思维和想象力所局限，误以为事物在此仍要遵循「一分努力、一分收获」的法则。
t_en: Life in the Dreamscape has never been difficult, but everyone is fettered by their mindset and imagination, mistakenly believing that they still have to obey the laws of "no pain, no gain."

t_ch: 殊不知，他们只需学会「观察」就能在梦中拥有自己所想的一切。
t_en: Little do they know, they can have everything they want here just by learning to "observe."

t_ch: 你看起来很有悟性。或许有朝一日，你能学会如何像我一样「观察」梦境。
t_en: You seem perceptive. Perhaps, you will master the way to "observe" the Dreamscape someday like I do.

t_ch: 什么样的行为会被家族制裁？
t_en: What kind of behavior is sanctioned by The Family?

t_ch: 凡是可能扰乱梦境秩序、或损害其他宾客权益的行为都会受到制裁。
t_en: Any behavior that may disrupt the Dreamscape's order or infringes upon the rights of other guests will be sanctioned.

t_ch: 这种「制裁」不是对现实中刑罚的直接映射，家族不会把施加痛苦当作制裁的手段——那会吓跑不少宾客。
t_en: This kind of sanction isn't a direct reflection of punishments in the real world. The Family doesn't inflict pain as sanction - that will drive many guests away.

t_ch: 我们的方法更加精密，悄无声息。它能有效阻断在梦境世界中犯罪的可能性，同时不会引起宾客的注意。
t_en: Instead, our method is more targeted and discreet. It can effectively prevent the possibility of crimes in the Dreamscape without drawing the attention of the guests.

t_ch: 这是可以说的吗？
t_en: Is that something you can divulge?

t_ch: 听上去不太正规…
t_en: Sounds fishy to me...

t_ch: 这并非什么秘密，任何宾客都有权知晓这些规则。
t_en: This isn't a secret. Any guest has the right to learn about these rules.

t_ch: 惠特克爵士露出一丝笑意。
t_en: Sir Whittaker cracks a subtle smile.

t_ch: 只是从来没人过问。
t_en: It's just that no one has ever asked about it.

t_ch: 你知道钟表匠吗？
t_en: Do you know the Watchmaker?

t_ch: 啊，「钟表匠」……
t_en: Oh, Watchmaker...

t_ch: 这个名字已经成为了一个符号，吸引了许多人前来匹诺康尼。从这个角度出发，我没有怨言。
t_en: The name has become an icon that draws many to Penacony. I have no complaints about that.

t_ch: 但我不会煽动任何人去探求「钟表匠」的秘密。忘记他，或许你的梦境之行会更加美满。
t_en: Yet, I won't talk anyone into probing the secrets of the Watchmaker. Your journey in the Dreamscape might be more fulfilling if you forget about him.

t_ch: 当然，这只是我的浅薄己见，无需放在心上。梦的美妙之处正在于此——它是自由的，没人能阻止你追寻埋藏于梦中的宝藏。
t_en: Of course, this is just my humble opinion, which you aren't obligated to heed. That's the beauty of a dream — you're free here, and no one can stop you from pursuing the treasures buried within.

t_ch: 没什么好聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 我还有事，先走一步。
t_en: I should get going.

t_ch: 祝你美梦成真。
t_en: May your dreams come true.

t_ch: 惠特克爵士面无表情，寒冽的目光穿透了你的脸庞。
t_en: Sir Whittaker's visage is expressionless, his cold gaze piercing through your face.

t_ch: 只要你愿意遵守梦境的规则，梦境就会不遗余力地给予你回馈。
t_en: As long as you're willing to abide by the rules of the Dreamscape, it will provide you with as much as it can.

t_ch: 有些人会告诉你，筑梦边境不欢迎游客到访。但在我看来，你出现在此处并无不妥。
t_en: Some may tell you that guests are not welcome at the Dream's Edge. But if you ask me, I'd say there's nothing wrong with you being here.

t_ch: 你可以继续上路，也可以提出问题。盛会之梦会响应你的所有诉求。
t_en: You can continue your journey or ask questions. The Dream of Festivities will respond to all your requests.

t_ch: 惠特克爵士面无表情，寒冽的目光穿透了你的脸庞。
t_en: Sir Whittaker's visage is expressionless, his cold gaze piercing through your face.

t_ch: 平静…在梦境里是极难达到的心境。
t_en: It's extremely hard to achieve a peaceful state of mind in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 宇宙间的智慧生命存在一个共性——他们或多或少都会被个人情绪所左右。在梦中，本就举足轻重的情绪会被放大、搅乱，随时可能淹没脆弱飘摇的「本我」。
t_en: All sentient beings in the universe have one thing in common — they are more or less at the mercy of their emotions. These emotions, which are already influential, are amplified and disrupted in dreams to the extent that they can easily overwhelm the vulnerable and wavering "id."

t_ch: 摆弄情绪是一项精细的技能，一如高超的钟表工艺。它的过程往往也伴随着很大的风险——为了你的个人的利益着想，我希望你已经充分理解了这一点……
t_en: Manipulating emotions is a delicate skill, much like the art of watchmaking. The process often comes with great risks. For your own sake, I hope you've fully grasped this concept...

t_ch: 惠特克爵士面无表情，寒冽的目光穿透了你的脸庞。
t_en: Sir Whittaker's visage is expressionless, his cold gaze piercing through your face.

t_ch: 家族能够在匹诺康尼取得如此成就，得益于从一开始就选对了方向。我们第一时间发现，肆意蔓延的情绪能量正在摧毁梦域，它们必须受到控制。
t_en: The Family has been so successful in Penacony because we chose the right direction right from the start. We were the first to discover the need to control the rampant emotional energy, as it was destroying the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 智慧生命拥有的情绪细腻、珍贵，但多数人没有意识到它危险的一面。世间所有罪行都源自情绪酝酿而成的冲动。
t_en: The emotions that sentient beings possess are delicate and precious, but most of us do not realize their dangerous side. All crimes in the world are driven by impulses that stem from emotions.

t_ch: 希望你对人的情绪足够了解，当心玩火自焚。
t_en: I hope you know enough about human emotions, or you'd be playing with fire.

t_ch: 惠特克爵士面无表情，深沉的目光穿透了你的脸庞。
t_en: Sir Whittaker's visage is expressionless, his brooding gaze piercing through your face.

t_ch: 我想给你讲一个故事。
t_en: Let me tell you a story.

t_ch: 很久、很久以前，早在家族开始着手建设盛会之星以前——那时的匹诺康尼深陷混乱之中，那时的梦境和噩梦基本可以划上等号。
t_en: A long, long time ago, even before The Family started the construction of the Planet of Festivities, Penacony was in a state of pandemonium. The Dreamscape at that time was no different from a nightmare.

t_ch: 因为各路机缘巧合，「家族」得以接手梦想之地。我们付出了巨大的努力，终于扭转了匹诺康尼在星间的恶劣的口碑，建成了你眼前的盛会之星。
t_en: Thanks to a string of coincidences, The Family was able to take over this land of dreams. We spared no effort and finally turned around Penacony's bad reputation in the universe, then transformed it into the Planet of Festivities that you now see.

t_ch: 家族能够在匹诺康尼取得如此成就，得益于从一开始就选对了方向。我们第一时间发现，肆意蔓延的情绪能量正在摧毁梦域，它们必须受到控制。
t_en: The Family has been so successful in Penacony because we chose the right direction right from the start. We were the first to discover the need to control the rampant emotional energy, as it was destroying the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 智慧生命拥有的情绪细腻、珍贵，但多数人没有意识到它危险的一面。世间所有罪行都源自情绪酝酿而成的冲动。
t_en: The emotions that sentient beings possess are delicate and precious, but most of us do not realize their dangerous side. All crimes in the world are driven by impulses that stem from emotions.

t_ch: 希望你对人的情绪足够了解，当心玩火自焚。
t_en: I hope you know enough about human emotions, or you'd be playing with fire.

t_ch: 惠特克爵士面无表情，寒冽的目光穿透了你的脸庞。
t_en: Sir Whittaker's visage is expressionless, his cold gaze piercing through your face.

t_ch: 家族能够在匹诺康尼取得如此成就，得益于从一开始就选对了方向。我们第一时间发现，肆意蔓延的情绪能量正在摧毁梦域，它们必须受到控制。
t_en: The Family has been so successful in Penacony because we chose the right direction right from the start. We were the first to discover the need to control the rampant emotional energy, as it was destroying the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 智慧生命拥有的情绪细腻、珍贵，但多数人没有意识到它危险的一面。世间所有罪行都源自情绪酝酿而成的冲动。
t_en: The emotions that sentient beings possess are delicate and precious, but most of us do not realize their dangerous side. All crimes in the world are driven by impulses that stem from emotions.

t_ch: 希望你对人的情绪足够了解，当心玩火自焚。
t_en: I hope you know enough about human emotions, or you'd be playing with fire.

t_ch: 惠特克爵士面无表情，锐利的目光穿透了你的脸庞。
t_en: Sir Whittaker's visage is expressionless, his sharp gaze piercing through your face.

t_ch: 相比权贵名流，我对那些选择孤注一掷的逐梦客更感兴趣。他们怀着不成功便成仁的决心来到这里，只为了追逐一个泡影。
t_en: Compared to the rich and famous, I'm more interested in the Dreamchasers who risk everything in their venture. It's all or nothing for them, and they're here to pursue something that might not be even real.

t_ch: 我想，你大概也是他们中的一员。
t_en: I suppose you're one of them.

t_ch: 惠特克爵士面无表情，锐利的目光穿透了你的脸庞。
t_en: Sir Whittaker's visage is expressionless, his sharp gaze piercing through your face.

t_ch: 我一直热衷于观察来到匹诺康尼的宾客。人究竟为什么会选择离开熟悉的现实并投身梦境，我直到今天都还在探究答案。
t_en: I've always been passionate about observing the guests who come to Penacony. Why would people choose to leave the reality they are familiar with for the Dreamscape? Till today, I'm still searching for the answer.

t_ch: 前来匹诺康尼的宾客中，有超过一半是在各自世界享有一定声誉的名流；其中更不乏富可敌星的超级富豪。
t_en: More than half of the guests here are well-known figures in their respective homeworlds. Some of them are even ultrarich tycoons with enough wealth to rival an entire planet.

t_ch: 他们在现实里享受着名利带来的所有好处。对于在星间挣扎求生的绝大多数而言，他们早已活在无法触及的梦中。
t_en: They are happily reaping the benefits of their fame in the real world. For the trillions of souls already struggling for survival in the cosmos, these individuals are already living in a dream.

t_ch: 他们抛下了自己享有的一切既得利益来到这里——这种选择的背后一定蕴含某种共性，某种逻辑。
t_en: Yet they leave behind the comforts of their lives to come here — there must be some underlying commonality and reasoning behind this choice.

t_ch: 当然，还有一个最简单的解释：他们只是被霓虹灯牌招引来的虫子，一群崇拜空洞享乐的乌合之众。
t_en: Of course, there's also a more straightforward explanation: They are merely moths drawn to bright neon signs. An ignorant gaggle of fools who worships meaningless hedonism.

t_ch: 惠特克爵士的脸上浮现一丝笑意，其中夹杂着嘲弄、蔑视和怜悯。
t_en: Sir Whittaker's face breaks into a smirk of mockery, disdain, and sympathy.

t_ch: 相比他们，我对那些真正怀揣梦想的逐梦客更感兴趣。他们选择孤注一掷，带着不成功便成仁的决心来到这里，只为了追逐一个泡影。
t_en: Compared to them, I'm more interested in the Dreamchasers who risk everything in their venture. It's all or nothing for them, and they're here to pursue something that might not be even real.

t_ch: 我想，你大概也是他们中的一员。收下这些艾迪恩代币吧——奋力逐梦的同时，也别忘了偶尔犒劳一下自己。
t_en: I suppose you're one of them. Please take these Aideen Tokens. Don't forget to treat yourself while you're striving for your dream.

t_ch: 惠特克爵士面无表情，锐利的目光穿透了你的脸庞。
t_en: Sir Whittaker's visage is expressionless, his sharp gaze piercing through your face.

t_ch: 相比无趣的贵宾名流，我对那些选择孤注一掷的逐梦客更感兴趣。他们怀着不成功便成仁的决心来到这里，只为了追逐一个泡影。
t_en: Compared to the boring rich and famous, I'm more interested in the Dreamchasers who risk everything in their venture. It's all or nothing for them, and they're here to pursue something that might not be even real.

t_ch: 我想，你大概也是他们中的一员。
t_en: I suppose you're one of them.

t_ch: 惠特克爵士面无表情，炽热的目光穿透了你的脸庞。
t_en: Sir Whittaker's visage is expressionless, his burning gaze piercing through your face.

t_ch: 你拥有的力量十分奇特。期待你在未来能够利用它做成一番大事。
t_en: You possess a very special power. I look forward to seeing you using it for something great in the future.

t_ch: 但愿你的所作所为能惊艳整个梦境，逐梦客。
t_en: May your achievements blow everyone in the Dreamscape away, Dreamchaser.

t_ch: 惠特克爵士面无表情，炽热的目光穿透了你的脸庞。
t_en: Sir Whittaker's visage is expressionless, his burning gaze piercing through your face.

t_ch: 你身上的能力…相当奇特。
t_en: The power you have... is very special.

t_ch: 不必紧张——我不会对你做什么，也不会深究它的来源。我只是感到好奇…拥有这份力量的人，未来会在梦想之地掀起什么样的波澜？
t_en: Relax. I won't do anything to you nor investigate the source of your power. I'm just curious... What kind of groundbreaking deeds will someone with that power achieve here in the future?

t_ch: 啊，实在有趣。你已经见过很多所谓的「大人物」了…未来的可能性不可限量。
t_en: Ah, this is very interesting. You've met a lot of the so-called big shots... The future contains endless possibilities for you.

t_ch: 也许…也许有机会。下一个「盛会之星」……
t_en: Maybe... Maybe there's a chance. The next "Planet of Festivities"...

t_ch: 啊，请别在意。以我个人的名义，请你收下这份礼金。就把它当作我对你未来的…投资。
t_en: Oh, I was just mumbling to myself. Here's a gift from me. Just see it... as my personal investment in your future.

t_ch: 惠特克爵士面无表情，炽热的目光穿透了你的脸庞。
t_en: Sir Whittaker's visage is expressionless, his burning gaze piercing through your face.

t_ch: 你拥有的力量十分奇特，我好奇你能在梦想之地掀起什么样的波澜。
t_en: You possess a very special power. I wonder what kind of groundbreaking deeds you can achieve here.

t_ch: 但愿你的所作所为能惊艳整个梦境，逐梦客。
t_en: May your achievements blow everyone in the Dreamscape away, Dreamchaser.

t_ch: 朋友，你喜欢悲剧还是喜剧？
t_en: Do you like stories with a sad ending or happy ending, my friend?

t_ch: 我个人比较喜欢前者，因为当「美」被精心敲碎时，那种崇高的悲壮总能令人们的灵魂战栗不已。
t_en: I personally prefer the former. When beauty is carefully shattered, the sublime devastation always leaves people's souls trembling.

t_ch: 当然，我同样欣赏那些优秀的喜剧作品。但我总觉得现在的自己就像是…某部劣质剧本中的可悲丑角一样。
t_en: Of course, I'm also a fan of well-made comedies. However, I always feel like... I'm currently a pathetic clown in some poorly-written script.

t_ch: 这种心情很难描述，失望、悲哀、恼怒…太多情绪搅混在一起，就像被随意倒进锅里的、不同口味的糖浆……
t_en: It's hard to describe the feeling. Disappointment, misery, anger... Too many emotions are mixed together, just like syrup of different flavors being randomly poured into a cauldron...

t_ch: 哦，对了，你找我有什么事？
t_en: Oh, by the way, what can I do for you?

t_ch: 你在这里做什么？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 但凡您鼻子上那两个窟…咳、抱歉，职业病犯了…我是说，如你所见，我在看风景。
t_en: If only the two holes above your nose are... Ahem, sorry, I do this for a living and I just can't help it... I mean, I'm enjoying the scenery, as you can see.

t_ch: 从这里远眺匹诺康尼大剧院姑且算是个不错的消遣。如果你也想借景消愁，我倒是不介意为你留个位置。
t_en: Taking in the view of the Penacony Grand Theater from here barely counts as a decent way to spend my free time. If you want to join, I don't mind saving you a spot.

t_ch: 那你愿意聊聊吗？
t_en: You want to chat?

t_ch: 可以，你想聊什么？
t_en: Sure. What do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 你看起来挺平静的。
t_en: You seem pretty calm?

t_ch: 是么？看来我该感到庆幸——那些百无一用的礼仪和教养早就在我骨子里生根发芽了。事实上，不，我现在心情很糟。
t_en: Do I? I should be glad, as those useless manners and etiquette entrenched in me are playing their parts. Honestly speaking, I'm having a bad mood.

t_ch: 我感觉自己受到了欺骗、侮辱…我为自己的盲目和轻率感到羞耻。
t_en: I feel cheated and humiliated... I'm ashamed of my ignorance and recklessness.

t_ch: 我跨越了几十个星系，只为找到一个伟大且纯净的灵魂，可结果……
t_en: I traveled across dozens of galaxies in search of a great and pure soul, but...

t_ch: 呵，所谓高尚无瑕的艺术不过是个谎言…这世上占据主导地位的还是迂腐与轻浮。
t_en: Hmph, the notion of noble and flawless art is nothing but a deception... Pedantry and frivolity still rule the world.

t_ch: 你是做什么的？
t_en: What do you do for a living?

t_ch: 我？我只是个品味和名声成反比的小小评论家——要是你有兴趣，倒是可以上我的个人网站逛逛。
t_en: Me? I'm just a small-time critic whose reputation doesn't reflect my taste. If you're interested, you can check out my personal website.

t_ch: 我从不过滤他人的评论。你在那里可以看到盛赞我的、攻讦我的、攻讦盛赞我的人的、攻讦攻讦我的人的、到处拱火的以及路过看热闹的……
t_en: I never filter the comments. You'll be able to see compliments, criticisms, criticisms for those who compliment me, criticisms for those who criticize me, trolls who instigate drama for fun, and bystanders...

t_ch: 如果哪天我打算转行当个小说家的话，光凭改编那些留言，怕不是就够写上好几本大部头了！
t_en: If I ever decide to become a novelist one day, I'll have enough material to write several books just by adapting those comments!

t_ch: 就先聊到这吧。
t_en: Let's end it here for now.

t_ch: 嗯，你自便。
t_en: No problem.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 迤逦灯火幽微，帷幕下影子沉睡……
t_en: The meandering lights grow faint, the shadows slumber beneath the curtain...

t_ch: 路易斯先是深吸一口气，然后缓缓吐出。
t_en: Louis takes a deep breath and lets it out slowly.

t_ch: …我真傻，真的。
t_en: ...I'm such a fool.

t_ch: 我本以为这是场朝圣之旅，却发现它不过是一幕荒诞喜剧，最大的丑角甚至还是我自己！
t_en: I thought I was on a pilgrimage, but this has turned out to be an absurd comedy, in which I'm the biggest clown!

t_ch: 果然我还是太过年轻，竟把鱼目错当珍珠。我为了她不远万里来到匹诺康尼，可她呢……
t_en: Indeed, I'm too young and naive, and I put my trust in the wrong person. I traveled thousands of miles to Penacony for her, but she...

t_ch: 你知道吗？当透过那些画作看她时，我窥见了星空与海岸、梦与自由、永恒与无限。
t_en: You know what? When I looked at her through the paintings, I saw the stars and the shores, dreams and freedom, eternity and infinity.

t_ch: 可当我真正见到她…却只觉星空寂灭、海洋枯涸。和她相处的每分每秒都显得如此漫长，如此煎熬……
t_en: But when I see her in person... I feel as though the stars have stopped shining, and the ocean has dried up. Every minute I spend with her feels so long and torturous...

t_ch: 苦修了二十余年的教养与礼仪，甚至难以支撑我在她面前敷衍地喝完一杯黑茶……
t_en: More than twenty years of manners and etiquette, and I was barely able to finish drinking a cup of tea in her presence...

t_ch: 何其可笑！我本寄希望于能找到一个独立而睿智的灵魂，可我最终得到的是什么？
t_en: How ironic! I had pinned my hopes on finding an independent and wise soul, but what do I get in the end?

t_ch: 一个囿于天分的奴隶、一杆攀附灵感的秃笔！
t_en: A slave confined by her talents! A bald quill without its own wits!

t_ch: 青年怔愣片刻，眨眨眼，突然对着你露出个微笑。
t_en: The young man is stuck in a trance for a brief moment. Then, he blinks and suddenly smiles at you.

t_ch: 真神奇…不知道为什么，在注视着你的时候我竟莫名感到平静……
t_en: How amazing... For some reason, I feel sense of tranquility when I look at you...

t_ch: 你知道吗？我从小就是个特别敏感的孩子。虽然我也一直在努力学习用理性思考，但到了关键的时候，那个感性的自我总是会占到上风。
t_en: You know, I've been particularly sensitive since I was little. Although I've been trying hard to think rationally, my emotional side always gets the better of me at critical moments.

t_ch: 我也尝试过各种各样的手段方法。就比如我手里这瓶——匹诺康尼特产的美梦糖浆，一小瓶就能让脑子安静一整天，既无副作用，也不会上瘾。
t_en: I've tried many methods, such as this bottle I'm holding — this Dream Syrup, Penacony's specialty. One small bottle can keep my mind calm for an entire day. It has no side effects and isn't addictive.

t_ch: 然而即便是这玩意，比起此刻你带给我的安宁感…唉，要是能把你绑走、随时带在身边就好了。
t_en: And yet, what it provides is nothing compared to the feelings of calmness and security that you are making me feel right now... Alas, if only I could kidnap you and keep you next to me at all times.

t_ch: …这句是个玩笑，你别紧张啊。不过说真的，你有空能多来和我聊聊天吗？
t_en: ...Don't take it so seriously. I'm just kidding. In all seriousness, would you mind coming by often to chat with me when you're free?

t_ch: 对了，这东西我一时半会儿也用不上，送给你好了。记得有空来找我哦？
t_en: By the way, I'm not going to use this thing any time soon, so you can have it. Remember to drop by when you're free!

t_ch: 真神奇…不知道为什么，在注视着你的时候我竟莫名感到平静……
t_en: How amazing... For some reason, I feel sense of tranquility when I look at you...

t_ch: 你知道吗？我从小就是个特别敏感的孩子。虽然我也一直在努力学习用理性思考，但到了关键的时候，那个感性的自我总是会占到上风。
t_en: You know, I've been particularly sensitive since I was little. Although I've been trying hard to think rationally, my emotional side always gets the better of me at critical moments.

t_ch: 我也尝试过各种各样的手段方法。就比如我手里这瓶——匹诺康尼特产的美梦糖浆，一小瓶就能让脑子安静一整天，既无副作用，也不会上瘾。
t_en: I've tried many methods, such as this bottle I'm holding — this Dream Syrup, Penacony's specialty. One small bottle can keep my mind calm for an entire day. It has no side effects and isn't addictive.

t_ch: 然而即便是这玩意，比起此刻你带给我的安宁感…唉，要是能把你绑走、随时带在身边就好了。
t_en: And yet, what it provides is nothing compared to the feelings of calmness and security that you are making me feel right now... Alas, if only I could kidnap you and keep you next to me at all times.

t_ch: …这句是个玩笑，你别紧张啊。不过说真的，你有空能多来和我聊聊天吗？
t_en: ...Don't take it so seriously. I'm just kidding. In all seriousness, would you mind coming by often to chat with me when you're free?

t_ch: 眼前的男人情绪肉眼可见地低落。
t_en: The man in front of you is clearly feeling depressed.

t_ch: 你听过「心灵测绘法」吗？
t_en: Have you heard of psychological topography?

t_ch: 嗯，没听过也不奇怪。那不是什么著名的理论，只是一种…流行于评论家群体间的无用技巧罢了。
t_en: Well, I'm not surprised if you haven't. It's not some famous theory. Just a... useless technique popular among critics.

t_ch: 简而言之，「心灵测绘法」等同于透过作品解读作者本人。不是粗暴地将作品展现出来的价值观套在作者身上，而是从潜意识流露出来的小细节入手进行剖析……
t_en: In essence, psychological topography is about interpreting the writer's personality through their works. Rather than directly associating the values shown in the works to the writer, we will analyze the subtle details that come from their subconscious...

t_ch: 曾经的我会用这种方法去评判艺术家，嘲讽那些我认为愚蠢的、抨击那些我以为顽固的……
t_en: I used to apply this method to evaluate artists, mocking those I deem foolish and criticizing those I think are too stubborn...

t_ch: 但是现在看来，既顽固又愚蠢的居然只有我自己——什么「心灵测绘法」，根本就是毫无理论依据的歪门邪道而已……
t_en: In hindsight, I can see that I'm the only one who's stubborn and foolish. The so-called psychological topography is just an unorthodox method without any theoretical basis...

t_ch: 或许他们说得对，我该重新找回对艺术的敬畏之心。强行把作品和艺术家本身的为人联系起来，这种行为压根毫无意义……
t_en: They're probably right — I should rediscover my reverence for art. It's pointless to force an association between an artist's personality and their works...

t_ch: 远处的灯火零星映在青年眼睛里，好似湖面上浮泛的粼粼月色。
t_en: The scattered lights from afar are reflected in the young man's eyes, resembling the shimmering moonlight on the surface of the lake.

t_ch: 在来到匹诺康尼之前，我从未想过自己竟会爱上这个地方。
t_en: Before coming to Penacony, I'd never thought I would fall in love with this place.

t_ch: 曾几何时，「盛会之星」这个名字给我的印象一直是浮夸轻佻、流于表面……
t_en: Once upon a time, the name "Planet of Festivities" struck me as pompous, frivolous, and superficial...

t_ch: …我一直将经典艺术视作一坛佳酿，发酵时间越久就越醇厚，韵味悠长；而匹诺康尼却到处都充斥着气泡丰盈的工业糖浆，欠缺历史的沉淀。
t_en: ...I always regarded classic art as a fine wine that became richer and more flavorful the longer it was fermented. Meanwhile, Penacony was a place filled with bubbly artificial syrup, lacking in the depth of history.

t_ch: 直到我真正置身于这座梦想之城，我才发现从前的自己有多么狭隘。你看，这座城市本身就是件绝妙的艺术品！
t_en: It wasn't until I stepped foot in this city of dreams did I realize how narrow-minded I used to be. Look! This city itself is a perfect piece of art!

t_ch: 家族接管了狂放的梦境，在混乱不堪的空间里造出了绝景，这本身不就是伟大创造力的显现吗？
t_en: The Family took charge of the untamed Dreamscape and created a masterpiece out of this anarchic space. Wasn't that a manifestation of great creativity?

t_ch: 真正踏足这座城市以后，我明白了一个道理——肤浅的不是这座城市，而是那些对艺术一窍不通、被消费主义毒害甚深的游客……
t_en: After coming here, I've learned one thing — it's not the city that's shallow, but those guests who know nothing about art and are brainwashed by toxic consumerism...

t_ch: 要是可以的话，我真恨不得在这里住上一个琥珀纪。哎，要怎么才能让我那几个兄弟姐妹同意呢……
t_en: I'd love to stay here for an Amber Era if I could. Alas, how am I supposed to convince my siblings to let me do it?

t_ch: 了解我们，您将拥有迅速累积财富的机会；信任我们，您会获得持续回报的未来。
t_en: Understand us, and you will own the chance to a swift and bountiful fortune. Trust us, and you will keep reaping rewards of the future.

t_ch: 我们执着于为客户创造可能。苜蓿草银行——您最好的理财伙伴。
t_en: We are dedicated to the creation of possibility for our clients. Alfalfa Bank — your most trusted financial partner.

t_ch: 嘿，好久不见！这位{F#姐}{M#哥}，还记得我吗？我是苜蓿草银行的基南啊！
t_en: Hey, it's been a long while! {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, remember me? I'm Keenan, from Alfalfa Bank!

t_ch: 记得，我们以前见过
t_en: Yes. We've met before.

t_ch: 果然！我就没认错，像您这样身份显赫的成功人士，简直就是茫茫人海中的明珠，到哪都能一眼看出来！
t_en: I knew it was you! A distinguished and successful figure like you always stands out from the crowd no matter where you go!

t_ch: 少套近乎，你谁啊
t_en: Stop pretending you know me. Who are you?

t_ch: 是这样的，我们苜蓿草银行信贷中心啊，最近刚推出了一款新型理财产品，特别适合您。
t_en: Well, our Alfalfa Bank Credit Center has recently launched a new financial product. I think it's a perfect fit for you.

t_ch: 年利率高达百分之三十四点八，稳定无风险，长线营收可观，不收取任何手续费，这边呢，想邀请您了解一下。
t_en: The annual interest rate is as high as <unbreak>34.8%</unbreak>. It's stable, risk-free, and will make a great long-term investment. No transaction fee is charged. Are you interested in learning more about it?

t_ch: 嗯？还有这等好事？
t_en: Oh? That sounds pretty good.

t_ch: 我没兴趣。
t_en: Not interested.

t_ch: …您先别急着走！我们给您推荐的产品呢，是不产生任何费用的，就算暂时不需要，也可以关注下，对您没坏处的。
t_en: ...Please don't go just yet! You don't have to pay any extra costs for this product. Even if you don't need it for the time being, there's no harm in learning more about it.

t_ch: 瞧见对面那座富丽堂皇的大楼了吧？不瞒您说，那位投资者正是我们的顶级客户，他来找我们的时候，还只是个刚来匹诺康尼的逐梦客，口袋里除了梦想什么都没有。
t_en: See that grand building across the street? That investor is one of our top clients! When he first approached us, he was just a Dreamchaser new to Penacony with nothing but dreams in his pockets.

t_ch: 苜蓿草银行慷慨接受了他，他也选择相信我们。现在您看，他拥有了这里的一切！
t_en: The Alfalfa Bank graciously accepted him, and he chose to trust us. Now, you can see he owns everything here!

t_ch: 每个人都值得一个机会。对我们而言是这样，对您也是如此！难道您宁可看着财富从指缝间溜走，也不愿稍微做个尝试，把未来紧紧攥在手中吗？
t_en: Everyone deserves a chance to succeed, and that's true for both you and us! Would you rather see money slipping through your fingers instead of trying to keep it in your grasp?

t_ch: 您费尽艰辛来到匹诺康尼，恐怕也不愿空手而归吧？
t_en: Having arrived at Penacony after such a tough journey, surely you wouldn't want to go home empty-handed?

t_ch: 哼，上次吹牛的时候，年利率还有百分之四十八呢…怎么，现在怕了？
t_en: Hmph, you said the interest rate was <unbreak>48%</unbreak> per annum the last time you bragged... What's it? Have you chickened out?

t_ch: 哎，话可不兴这么说啊！我先代表苜蓿草银行警告你，造谣是要负责任的！
t_en: Look, you shouldn't make baseless allegations like that! I must warn you on behalf of the Alfalfa Bank that you can be held accountable for the rumors you spread!

t_ch: 您是上流人士，可别被这些闲杂人员的话蒙了眼！刚才谈到的理财产品，要是有意……
t_en: You're a member of the upper crust. Don't be swayed by people who know nothing! If you're interested in the financial product I mentioned earlier...

t_ch: 他是你们的客户吗？
t_en: Is he your client?

t_ch: 他好像知道些什么…
t_en: He seems to know something...

t_ch: 呃，或许是…具体情况，得等我回去查查资料。您知道的，我们的客户很多，总会有那么些人缺少上进心，最后落得穷困潦倒。谁也救不了他们。
t_en: Er, perhaps... I'll have to check the files for details. As you already know, we have a lot of clients, some of whom may be more laissez-faire in the attitudes and end up in poverty. No one can save them.

t_ch: 别管他了，我们继续聊……
t_en: Ignore him. Let's keep chatting...

t_ch: 骂谁穷呢！嗯？要不是信了你们的鬼话，我至于沦落到给人打工吗！
t_en: Who are you calling poor!? If it weren't for your fancy talk, I wouldn't be stuck working for someone else!

t_ch: 来来，看清楚了。这哪张合约上写的不是请您放心，哪张现在不是废纸，不是赔得血本无归，连瞅一眼都觉得心痛？
t_en: Here, take a good look. All these agreements state it's all safe, don't worry, and now they're all nothing but scrap paper. I've been made a pauper! It hurts to even glance at these papers!

t_ch: 这个就……
t_en: This is...

t_ch: 好吧，你明白了，谁没在路上碰到过几个推销员呢，大家都不容易。
t_en: Now you understand. Who hasn't run into a salesperson or two along the way, eh? Everyone's just trying to hustle.

t_ch: …别走呀！要是对理财没信心，我还有保险套餐可以推荐！再给我两分钟就好！
t_en: ...No, wait! If you have no confidence in our financial policies, I still have an insurance package to recommend! Please give me another two minutes!

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to chat with you.

t_ch: 您主动搭话，莫非是对我们的理财产品感兴趣？
t_en: Since you want to talk, does that mean you're interested in our financial products?

t_ch: 请随便问！我都听着呢，都听着……
t_en: Ask away! I'm all ears, all ears...

t_ch: 关于理财产品…
t_en: About your financial products...

t_ch: 我说的可都是实话！年利率高达百分之二十三点六，绝对平稳无风险，数位金融专家轮班观测研究，只为守护您的资金安全。您真的不考虑吗？
t_en: Everything I say is true! With absolutely stable and risk-free annual returns as high as <unbreak>23.6%</unbreak>, analyzed and approved by countless financial experts all for your financial security. Are you really not going to consider it?

t_ch: 在这座梦想之城，每个人都有向上走的路，缺少的只是机遇…和些微的运气。
t_en: In this city of dreams, everyone wants to scale the ladder of success, but they lack opportunity... and a little luck.

t_ch: 我们愿意为您提供这样的窗口。要是不抓住机会，落到底层可就怨不得人了。
t_en: And we would like to provide that window of opportunity to you. If you miss out, don't blame us for landing flat on your feet.

t_ch: 关于保险套餐…
t_en: About the insurance package...

t_ch: 担心突如其来的事故难以承担吗？想为家人留下意外后的保障吗？欢迎选购我们新推出的人身安全险套餐！苜蓿草银行——为您的未来保驾护航。
t_en: Concerned about sudden happenings that overweigh your burdens? Want your family to be financially secure if you meet with an accident? You're more than welcome to explore our newly-launched life security package! Alfalfa Bank — navigating secure passage for your future.

t_ch: 尽管在匹诺康尼，您的安全会得到家族最大限度的保护——但看看最近的情况，谁敢确定不会碰上个三长两短呢？只需投入极少的资金，就能轻松获得高达数万倍的赔偿！
t_en: On Penacony, your safety will be completely safeguarded by The Family. Though, seeing the current climate, who knows when one would run into an accident or two? But for just a meager sum, you will be ensured a payout that's over ten thousand times!

t_ch: 不瞒您说，其实我自己也买了这项保险套餐…相信我，总有能用上的一天！
t_en: To be honest, even I bought this insurance package myself. It'll definitely come in useful some day!

t_ch: 关于结算方式…
t_en: About how returns are distributed...

t_ch: 啊，您是想问产品收益会以什么形式发放吧？别担心，我们的交易全程由家族担保，利润结算可以按您指定的任意方式进行。
t_en: Ah, you wish to ask about how the proceeds are going to be distributed to you? Don't worry. Our entire transaction process is guaranteed by The Family. You can choose how you want to receive the interest earned.

t_ch: 苜蓿钞、艾迪恩代币、信用点，甚至外星球的货币都能使用。
t_en: Alfalfa Coins, Aideen Tokens, credits, we even use currencies of foreign worlds.

t_ch: …当然，被家族明令禁止流通的金表钞除外。
t_en: ...Of course, apart from Clock Credits, of which The Family has forbidden circulation.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: 要是这样…能够帮助您坚定对我行理财产品信心的话，我…很乐意。
t_en: If... it can instill greater confidence in our financial products, I... am more than happy.

t_ch: 请手脚快点，街上人多，有点…不好意思。
t_en: Please make it quick. There are many people around, and I'm... a little embarrassed.

t_ch: 我没什么兴趣。
t_en: I'm not interested.

t_ch: 惠特克爵士面无表情，寒冽的目光穿透了你的脸庞。
t_en: Sir Whittaker's visage is expressionless, his cold gaze piercing through your face.

t_ch: 还有几款产品可以给您推荐呢，这是我的名片，要不您先拿着？诶，您走好！
t_en: I have some other products I can recommend. Here's my card. Why not take it with you first? Oh, have a nice day!

t_ch: 了解我们，您将拥有迅速累积财富的机会；信任我们，您会获得持续回报的未来。
t_en: Understand us, and you will own the chance to a swift and bountiful fortune. Trust us, and you will keep reaping rewards of the future.

t_ch: 我们执着于为客户创造可能。苜蓿草银行——您最好的理财伙伴。
t_en: We are dedicated to the creation of possibility for our clients. Alfalfa Bank — your most trusted financial partner.

t_ch: 嘿，又见面了！这位{F#姐}{M#哥}，还记得我吗？我是苜蓿草银行的基南啊！
t_en: Heyo, we meet again! {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, remember me? I'm Keenan, from Alfalfa Bank!

t_ch: 这次我真记得了
t_en: I actually remember you this time

t_ch: 少套近乎，你谁啊
t_en: Stop pretending you know me. Who are you?

t_ch: 哎呀，刚才的事是我们的错，没做好过往客户的维护工作，让您看笑话了。
t_en: Oh dear, it was our fault just now for not looking after our past clients well. We're truly ashamed.

t_ch: 呼呼…要发财了！许多人梦寐以求的机会，终于落到了我的头上！
t_en: Hahaha, I'm gonna be rich! The opportunity that many can only dream of... is finally upon me!

t_ch: 呼呼…要发财了！许多人梦寐以求的机会，终于落到了我的头上！
t_en: Hahaha, I'm gonna be rich! The opportunity that many can only dream of... is finally upon me!

t_ch: 喂，旁边的，站这么近，是想偷听我成功的秘诀吗？告诉你也无妨，反正你做不到。
t_en: Hey, you there, you're standing too close. Trying to learn the secrets to my success? Lemme tell you then. It doesn't matter, because you won't be able to replicate it.

t_ch: 好好听着，前几天营业部来了位大客户。具体有多大…这么跟你说吧，负责接待的那个小子，从他身上捞到了一整年的业绩！啧，明明资历比我浅，真是阳寿换来的运气！
t_en: Listen up. A major client came by the sales department a few days ago. How major? Let me put it this way — the kid who serviced this client hit his KPI for the entire year! Tsk... I've got years of more experience than that brat! Did he trade in his life for this sort of luck or what?

t_ch: 然而，就在昨天，那个客户指名了我，对，就是我！做他的专属代理人！你说，这是不是天上掉下来的馅饼！哪有这种好事！
t_en: Still, just yesterday, that client specially asked for me! Yes, me! To be his exclusive manager! Have I truly lucked out or what! How could this be happening to me!?

t_ch: 我可得好好把这位爷供着…接送上下班是小事，洗衣做饭烧水搓澡都不在话下！
t_en: I'll have to treat this client like a king... chauffeuring him to and from work? No problem! Laundry, cooking, scrubbing his back, all part of the day's work!

t_ch: 你还没走？哦，我终于明白你要的是什么了。拿去，今天我开心！
t_en: You're still here? Oh, I've finally realized what you wanted. Here, take this. I'm in a good mood today!

t_ch: 呼呼…要发财了！许多人梦寐以求的机会，终于落到了我的头上！
t_en: Hahaha, I'm gonna be rich! The opportunity that many can only dream of... is finally upon me!

t_ch: 喂，旁边的，站这么近，是想偷听我成功的秘诀吗？告诉你也无妨，反正你做不到。
t_en: Hey, you there, you're standing too close. Trying to learn the secrets to my success? Lemme tell you then. It doesn't matter, because you won't be able to replicate it.

t_en: Listen up. A major client came by the sales department a few days ago. How major? Let me put it this way — the kid who serviced this client hit his KPI for the entire year! Tsk... I've got years of more experience than that brat! Did he trade in his life for this sort of luck or what?

t_ch: 然而，就在昨天，那个客户指名了我，对，就是我！做他的专属代理人！你说，这是不是天上掉下来的馅饼！哪有这种好事！
t_en: Still, just yesterday, that client specially asked for me! Yes, me! To be his exclusive manager! Have I truly lucked out or what! How could this be happening to me!?

t_ch: 我可得好好把这位爷供着…接送上下班是小事，洗衣做饭烧水搓澡都不在话下！
t_en: I'll have to treat this client like a king... chauffeuring him to and from work? No problem! Laundry, cooking, scrubbing his back, all part of the day's work!

t_ch: 去你的苜蓿草银行！整天业绩业绩，都是放屁！信不信我把你们一个个都崩了！
t_en: Curse you and your Alfalfa Bank! Work work work all day, it's all rubbish! You think I won't end all of you!

t_ch: 去你的苜蓿草银行！整天业绩业绩，都是放屁！信不信我把你们一个个都崩了！
t_en: Curse you and your Alfalfa Bank! Work work work all day, it's all rubbish! You think I won't end all of you!

t_ch: 当我不知道背后都在搞什么勾当呢。那群高管看起来人模人样的，都是吸血鬼！白天开会研究骗钱，夜里到处寻欢作乐。搞什么末位淘汰，第一个就该把你们开了！
t_en: You think I don't know what's going on behind all this? Those suits all pretending to be kind and caring — bloodsuckers, all of 'em! Formulating all sorts of scams in daytime meetings, and seeking decadent pleasure at night. And firing the lowest performers? How about I fire you lot!

t_ch: 还有那个浑身劣质香水味的客户，见面就让我亲他手背，咋的不敢让我叫他一声父亲呢？穿件黑西装就真把自己当个大人物了不是？
t_en: And that client with the nauseatingly foul perfume, making me kiss the back of his hand every time we meet. How about I just call him my uncle and let him treat me like a dog? Think you're some big shot just because you're decked out in a snazzy black suit?

t_ch: 成功，发财，奋斗，梦想…还搁这喂公子吃饼。我呸！
t_en: Success, wealth, hustling, dreams... and I still have to treat this man like he's my master. Phooey!

t_ch: 送给你！我现在看到这个就恶心！
t_en: Here, take it! The mere sight of this makes me want to throw up!

t_ch: 去你的苜蓿草银行！整天业绩业绩，都是放屁！信不信我把你们一个个都崩了！
t_en: Curse you and your Alfalfa Bank! Work work work all day, it's all rubbish! You think I won't end all of you!

t_ch: 当我不知道背后都在搞什么勾当呢。那群高管看起来人模人样的，都是吸血鬼！白天开会研究骗钱，夜里到处寻欢作乐。搞什么末位淘汰，第一个就该把你们开了！
t_en: You think I don't know what's going on behind all this? Those suits all pretending to be kind and caring — bloodsuckers, all of 'em! Formulating all sorts of scams in daytime meetings, and seeking decadent pleasure at night. And firing the lowest performers? How about I fire you lot!

t_ch: 还有那个浑身劣质香水味的客户，见面就让我亲他手背，咋的不敢让我叫他一声父亲呢？穿件黑西装就真把自己当个大人物了不是？
t_en: And that client with the nauseatingly foul perfume, making me kiss the back of his hand every time we meet. How about I just call him my uncle and let him treat me like a dog? Think you're some big shot just because you're decked out in a snazzy black suit?

t_ch: 成功，发财，奋斗，梦想…还搁这喂公子吃饼。我呸！
t_en: Success, wealth, hustling, dreams... and I still have to treat this man like he's my master. Phooey!

t_ch: 就是说，为什么活着这么难呢？
t_en: Which is to say: Why is living so hard?

t_ch: 就是说，为什么活着这么难呢？
t_en: Which is to say: Why is living so hard?

t_ch: 不是只要努力就能发财吗，我明明…付出了这么多，千里迢迢到这里，花大价钱和家族的人处好关系，入住酒店都是用房产做抵押的……
t_en: Doesn't hard work translate into wealth? I obviously... gave up so much, traveled all the way here, spent a fortune getting cozy with The Family, shacked up in this hotel using my own house as collateral...

t_ch: 可现在…什么都捞不着，产品没人买，住宿费用倒越来越高，想让孩子来玩也付不起……
t_en: But now... I've come away empty-handed. I've sold nothing, racked up a huge hotel bill, and I can't even take my kids on holiday...

t_ch: 匹诺康尼不是梦想之城吗…我、我也有自己的梦想啊！
t_en: Isn't Penacony supposed to be the city of dreams...? I, I also have my own dreams!

t_ch: 哈…哈哈哈哈……
t_en: Ha... Hahaha...

t_ch: 不用怕。哈…哈哈，我都准备好了。放心，不会让你们吃苦的。哈哈哈……
t_en: All right, fear not. Ha... Haha, I'm all ready now. Don't worry. You won't suffer. Hahaha...

t_ch: 情绪崩溃的基南突然狂笑起来，有张花花绿绿的纸掉落在他脚边。
t_en: The emotionally broken Keenan suddenly laughs maniacally, a piece of floral green paper falling by his feet.

t_ch: （咦，这个是……）
t_en: (Huh? That's...)

t_ch: 就是说，为什么活着这么难呢？
t_en: Which is to say: Why is living so hard?

t_ch: 不是只要努力就能发财吗，我明明…付出了这么多，千里迢迢到这里，花大价钱和家族的人处好关系，入住酒店都是用房产做抵押的……
t_en: Doesn't hard work translate into wealth? I obviously... gave up so much, traveled all the way here, spent a fortune getting cozy with The Family, shacked up in this hotel using my own house as collateral...

t_ch: 可现在…什么都捞不着，产品没人买，住宿费用倒越来越高，想让孩子来玩也付不起……
t_en: But now... I've come away empty-handed. I've sold nothing, racked up a huge hotel bill, and I can't even take my kids on holiday...

t_ch: 匹诺康尼不是梦想之城吗…我、我也有自己的梦想啊！
t_en: Isn't Penacony supposed to be the city of dreams...? I, I also have my own dreams!

t_ch: 哈…哈哈哈哈……
t_en: Ha... Hahaha...

t_ch: 不用怕。哈…哈哈，我都准备好了。放心，不会让你们吃苦的。哈哈哈……
t_en: All right, fear not. Ha... Haha, I'm all ready now. Don't worry. You won't suffer. Hahaha...

t_ch: 证书就是人生的意义！
t_en: Certificates are what determine meaning in life!

t_ch: 噢，你也对我刚考的汽水品鉴技能证感兴趣吧？我知道，你肯定想看。
t_en: Oh, you're interested in the soda appraisal skill certificate I just obtained as well? I know, you want a peek.

t_ch: 家里的长辈说，厉害的人能考很多证书，拿到很多很多奖章！所以，我也要收集很多很多很多的证书，还有奖章，来证明我是个厉害的人！
t_en: My elders tell me that the really smart ones can get certified for a ton of stuff, and get plenty of medals! Which is why I'm also collecting plenty of certificates and medals, to prove that I'm smart too!

t_ch: 你瞅瞅，口袋里都是。嘿嘿，很努力才弄到的…我做得还行吧？
t_en: Look, my pocket's full of them. Heehee, you have to work hard to get these... I'm not too shabby, eh?

t_ch: 那当然！
t_en: Definitely!

t_ch: 你能明白！你是我最好的朋友！我要把所有的证书和奖章…都给你看！
t_en: You get it! You're my bestie! I'm gonna show all my certificates and medals here... to you!

t_ch: 我们橡木家系的成员，绝不会亏待朋友！
t_en: We Oaks would never do our friends dirty!

t_ch: 有什么用呢？
t_en: What's the point of these?

t_ch: 当、当然有用了！要是没有那些证书，我该怎么跟家里人证明，说我有用呢！
t_en: O—Of course there's a point! If I didn't have these certificates, how would I prove to my family that I'm useful!

t_ch: 是不是弄混了。
t_en: You sure you got it right?

t_ch: 有吗，我理理…证书是很努力才能得到的东西，受到认可，大家都喜欢。获得证书，说明我很努力，我得到了认可，大家都喜欢我。
t_en: Have I not? Let me think... A certificate is obtained through hard work. Everyone likes someone who's recognized. Getting a certificate means I've worked hard and gained recognition, then everyone will like me.

t_ch: 好像没问题……
t_en: It looks fine...

t_ch: 家里人是指谁。
t_en: Who's this family you're talking about?

t_ch: 啊，糟、糟糕，大伯交待过我，不能随便说自己是橡木家系的成员……
t_en: Ah, o—oh no, Uncle told me never to reveal that I'm a member of the Oak Family...

t_ch: 不管怎么样，证书…还有奖章，都是很重要的！我都给你看！
t_en: Anyway, the certificates... and the medals, they're really important! I'll show all of them to you!

t_ch: 你被迫听笨罐先生讲解起了他获得证书的艰辛历程，时间流逝得如同水珠在冰过的汽水瓶表面凝结那般缓慢。承认吧，要是可以，你也想考张汽水品鉴技能证。
t_en: You are forced to sit through Mr. Piggy Bank's monologue on his arduous journey to acquire all his certificates. Time trickles as slow as pearls of condensed droplets inching their way down the side of a chilled soda can. Admit it, if you could, you also want to try sitting for a soda appraisal certificate.

t_ch: 球笼轨道维修资格、剧院报幕员证、训物大赛银奖、还有酒店下水道疏通技能证……
t_en: Spheroid rail repair, theater announcer, object training silver medal, and hotel drainage unclogging certificates...

t_ch: 嘿嘿，会耐心听我说的，你是第一个！好朋友！
t_en: Heheh, you're the first one who's ever had the patience to sit through my monologue! You're a good friend!
